name: Speedy Cash
description_short: Speedy Cash Round Rock, Texas — Speedy Cash is a short-term lender offering payday loans up to $425, lines of credit up to $4,000, and title loans up to...
description_long:
Speedy Cash is a national short-term lending chain with a retail presence across the United States, including this location at 5126 Summer Ave in Memphis, Tennessee. The company operates under a brick-and-mortar model emphasizing in-person service, though online applications are also available. The Memphis Summer Avenue store is open six days a week with extended hours on Mondays and Fridays, and offers bilingual customer service in both English and Spanish.

This location offers three personal loan products: Payday Loans up to $425, which are repaid on or around the borrower's next payday; Lines of Credit up to $4,000, which allow flexible draws and repayments over time; and Title Loans up to $4,000, secured by the borrower's vehicle title and requiring an in-store appraisal. Beyond lending, the store also provides money orders, wire transfers, check cashing, and Green Dot Visa Debit Card services, making it a multi-service financial retail outlet.

What distinguishes this location is its emphasis on in-person guidance throughout the loan process. Staff are available to walk applicants through product options, assist with paperwork, and answer repayment questions on the spot. The store's bilingual support (English and Spanish hotlines) and Saturday hours add accessibility for a broader customer base. The co-location of money services alongside lending products means customers can handle multiple financial tasks in a single visit.

Honestly, Speedy Cash caters to borrowers with limited access to conventional credit, and its products carry the high costs typical of the payday and title loan industry. The payday loan ceiling of $425 is modest, and repayment timelines are short. Title loans require surrendering a clear vehicle title, which carries real risk if repayment becomes difficult. The website's store description also contains a copy error referencing 'Check Into Cash' — a sister brand under the same parent company — which reflects inconsistent quality control. Borrowers should read Tennessee-specific rates and terms carefully before applying.
address: 907 N Interstate Hwy 35 B, Round Rock, TX 78664

company_info.headquarters: 907 N Interstate Hwy 35 B, Round Rock, TX 78664

pros: Three distinct loan products covering a range of amounts: payday ($425), line of credit ($4,000), and title loans ($4,000)
Line of credit product allows flexible draws and repayments — credit limit restores as you pay down
In-store staff available to walk applicants through the full process including document review and repayment questions
Bilingual customer service with separate English (1-888-333-1360) and Spanish (1-855-734-0111) hotlines
Multiple financial services in one location: money orders, wire transfers, check cashing, Green Dot Visa Debit Card
Both in-store and online application paths available
Open six days a week with extended hours Monday and Friday (9am–7pm)
cons: Payday loan maximum is capped at $425 — low for larger emergency needs
Payday loans due on next payday, giving very little repayment flexibility
Title loans require a clear vehicle title and in-store appraisal — vehicle is at risk if you default
Store webpage contains a copy error referencing competitor brand 'Check Into Cash,' suggesting quality control issues
best_for: Memphis residents needing a small emergency cash advance before their next paycheck
Borrowers with a paid-off vehicle who need up to $4,000 and can tolerate secured lending risk
Customers who prefer in-person guidance and want to ask questions during the application process
Spanish-speaking borrowers who need native-language phone support
diagnosis: Best for Memphis-area consumers who need fast cash and have exhausted bank or credit union options, particularly those who want in-person help navigating loan products. The main caveat is that payday and title loans carry high costs and short repayment windows — borrowers who cannot repay quickly can end up in a debt cycle, and title loan defaulters risk losing their vehicle.
